company.what_they_do: Delos Labs is a personal portfolio of applied finance and technology projects by Pedro. The work spans quantitative finance, LLM-driven financial analysis, blockchain/DeFi, and multi-agent AI systems — all with a strong focus on the Brazilian market. Projects include: (1) applying LLMs (GPT-4) to analyze Brazilian public companies' financial statements and predict earnings; (2) a decentralized on-chain consórcio (Brazilian group-savings product) built with smart contracts and deployed on LACHAIN Testnet; (3) a VIX-equivalent volatility index for Brazil's B3 market, built from options chain data; (4) a multi-agent AI hedge-fund system for Brazilian equities using the BRAPI API; and (5) 'DELOS' — a blockchain-based oracle and tokenized securities system for Brazilian market infrastructure (content truncated on the site).
raw_notes: Portfolio site, not a funded startup — appears to be a solo practitioner/builder showcasing project work.
All five listed projects are Brazil-focused financial engineering or fintech experiments.

Project 1 (LLM_FinancialAnalysis): Replicates methodology from academic paper 'Financial Statement Analysis with Large Language Models' adapted to Brazilian public companies. Uses OpenAI GPT-4 via API, SQL database of income statements/balance sheets, custom prompt templates, multi-year trend analysis. Key insight: prompt structure and data preparation materially affect LLM financial reasoning quality.

Project 2 (Decentralized Consórcio): Proof-of-concept smart contract version of the traditional Brazilian consórcio savings product. Deployed and verified on LACHAIN Testnet (contract visible on explorer). Key insight: surfaces gap between protocol design and production-ready deployment — highlights need for test coverage and threat modeling.

Project 3 (Brazilian VIX): Built a VIX-equivalent volatility index for Brazil's B3 market from scratch, adapting CBOE VIX methodology to messy/incomplete Brazilian options data. Used PostgreSQL, options chain API, daily calculations, compared against Ibovespa actual volatility. Key insight: pragmatic approximations beat strict textbook adherence when data is inconsistent.

Project 4 (Agents Orchestrated Fund): Multi-agent AI system for Brazilian stock analysis, adapted from virattt's hedge-fund-agent-team, using BRAPI API. Each agent specializes in a different aspect of financial analysis. Key insight: agent specialization improves modularity but system is sensitive to prompt design; best used as analytical copilot.
Project 5 (DELOS): Blockchain-based oracle and tokenized securities system for Brazilian market infrastructure. Description truncated in provided content.
The site's framing emphasizes 'real-world constraints, technical trade-offs, and applied system design' — pragmatic, engineering-minded tone throughout.
The project named 'DELOS' shares its name with the organization, suggesting this may be the flagship or namesake project.
